title: Advertising and marketing associate professionals
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. (Based on 38 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Record names, addresses, purchases, and reactions of prospects contacted, and obtain customer information for processing orders.
Adjust sales scripts to target the needs and interests of specific individuals, and conduct client or market surveys to gather information about potential customers.
Answer telephone calls from potential customers solicited through advertisements and follow up on initial sales contacts.
Contact businesses or individuals to solicit sales for goods or services, explain products or services and prices, and answer customer questions.
Establish relationships with community representatives to enhance public relations, and prepare speeches or communications to promote clients.
Plan or conduct market research to test products, determine potential for success, and monitor market conditions and competitors.
Develop marketing campaigns in various media for products, organizations, or individuals, and coordinate promotional activities with advertising agencies.
Prepare press releases and organizational publications for internal or external audiences, maintaining favorable public perceptions.
Engage in direct communication with customers, providing detailed information and support regarding products and services to resolve issues post-purchase.
Identify prospective customers using various leads, and generate customer leads to develop new accounts.
Read scripts and confer with producers to determine the types and numbers of performers required for a given production.
Select performers for roles or submit lists of suitable performers to producers or directors for final selection.
Audition and interview performers to match their attributes to specific roles and increase the pool of available talent.
Negotiate contract agreements with performers, agents, or production companies.
Arrange for or design screen tests or auditions for prospective performers.
Answer customers' questions about products, prices, availability, or credit terms.
Maintain customer records using automated systems.
Prepare sales presentations or proposals to explain product specifications or applications.
Visit establishments to evaluate needs or promote product or service sales.
Perform management activities, such as budgeting, scheduling, planning, and marketing.
Confer with clients to determine objectives, budget, background information, and presentation approaches.
Collaborate with Web, multimedia, or art design staffs to create multimedia websites or other internet content.
Develop and implement procedures for identifying advertising needs.
Measure the effectiveness of marketing, advertising, and communications programs and strategies.
Prepare reports of findings, illustrating data graphically and translating complex findings into written text.
Collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, needs, and buying habits to identify potential markets and product demand.
Collaborate with other marketing staff to integrate and complement marketing strategies across multiple sales channels.
Conduct market research analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and competitor performance affecting product promotion.
Develop advertising campaigns for clients, working with creative and art directors to present advertising information effectively.
Write and prepare promotional plans, sales literature, media kits, and sales contracts, using computer tools.
Consult with clients to develop strategies for their careers and explain actions taken on their behalf.
Negotiate with various parties regarding clients' contracts and rights, ensuring their best interests are represented.
Identify new advertising markets and propose products to serve them, enhancing overall marketing effectiveness.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to customers to sell new advertising programs and protect existing business.
Consult with clients, vendors, and personnel in other departments to discuss and formulate estimates and resolve issues.
Confer with engineers, architects, owners, contractors, and subcontractors on changes and adjustments to cost estimates.
Prepare estimates used by management for planning, organizing, and scheduling work, including estimates for selecting vendors or subcontractors.
Produce written summary reports of financial research results and interpret outcomes of financial analysis procedures.
Verify customer and order information for correctness and check it against previous information as needed.
Review orders for completeness according to reporting procedures, and forward incomplete orders for further processing.
Compile data from records to prepare periodic reports and documents, including invoices or correspondence regarding service complaints.
Gather records pertinent to specific problems, ensure their completeness and accuracy, and respond to customer complaints as needed.
Compose letters in reply to correspondence concerning merchandise requests, damage claims, billing issues, or service complaints.
Maintain information on clients, vendors, and travel packages, ensuring accurate documentation and updated records.
Create marketing or outreach media, such as brochures or websites, to effectively communicate service offers or sustainability initiatives.
Develop or revise business plans for online business, research products or markets, and identify opportunities for merchandising.
Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends or threats to security, assets, and information systems.
Develop disaster recovery plans for critical locations, ensuring continuity of operations and governance during disruptions.
Create scenarios to reestablish operations from various types of business disruptions, identifying key recovery strategies.
Create, manage, or automate orders or invoices using order management or invoicing software.
Participate in online forums or conferences to stay updated on online retailing trends.
Upload digital media, such as photos or videos, to online storefronts or auction sites.
Order or purchase merchandise to maintain optimal inventory levels and manage shipping supplies.
Determine and set product prices and formulate pricing policies based on profitability requirements.
Disclose merchant information and terms of transactions in both online and offline materials.
Create or maintain a database of customer accounts and keep records of purchases and sales.
Coordinate sales promotion activities, such as preparing merchandise displays and advertising copy.
Monitor sales staff performance and listen to customer complaints to ensure satisfaction.
Conduct security audits for vulnerabilities and design policies to ensure adequate security measures in projects.
Confer with company officials to develop methods to increase sales and promote business.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in inventory-taking, sales, and specific services.
Attend meetings to exchange product information and coordinate activities with other departments.
Prepare sales and inventory reports for management and budget departments, monitoring costs incurred by staff.
Plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities, ensuring deadlines are met and monitoring milestones.
Negotiate with project stakeholders to obtain necessary resources or materials for projects.
Communicate with stakeholders to determine project requirements and identify project needs.
Develop or update project plans including objectives, schedules, and staffing requirements.
Monitor the performance of project team members, providing feedback and resolving any issues that arise.
